title: Got my first unsolicited advice before I even have a treatment plan. DCIS + Microinvasive DC HER2+
body:
36F here.  About a week ago my biopsy results came back HER2+ which has sped up all my testing. Its a real mixed blessing as I'm so impatient to know what my treatments and prognosis are but I know HER2+ is aggressive and the area of my breast affected with clogged up ducts is like 8cm. I went in for genetics testing as one of my 6 doctor appointments this week and the guy drawing my blood told me I should cut out all sugar because "sugar feeds cancer" and go vegan like him. He also mentioned that I should really up my fruit intake. I thought about asking him if he knew what fructose was. But instead I just let him know that my doctor told me I can't eat tofu more than once a week anymore which is really sad because I really like tofu so yeah I'm not going vegan. 

I had my son last year and I was really enjoying people not giving me unsolicited pregnancy advice anymore but it looks like I'm in for another round of strangers telling me what I should and shouldn't do. Did y'all experience a lot of unsolicited advice?
